description: Field-by-field catalog for all 26 fields in the Compatibility Rule Schema. Documents purpose, allowed values, examples, validation rules, and cross-field dependencies for each field.

fields.purpose: Serves as the globally unique, stable primary key for every CompatibilityRule. Used as the node key in Neo4j, the document ID in Qdrant, and the reference identifier in all cross-system citations and sequence chains.
validation_rules: Must match pattern ^CRULE-[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]{3,}$
Must be unique across all rules in the system
Must not be reused or recycled after deletion or archival
Must not contain lowercase characters or spaces
relationships_to_other_fields: sequence_prerequisite_ref references this field in another rule to build sequence chains
Immutable after creation; no other field change can modify rule_id

fields.purpose: Classifies the semantic nature of the rule, selecting the evaluation model, required field set, operator vocabulary, severity constraints, and minimum confidence threshold that apply to this rule.
validation_rules: Must be one of the 6 registered rule types
Must not be null or empty
Determines which fields are conditionally required
relationships_to_other_fields: Governs minimum threshold for confidence field
Governs allowed values for severity field
Determines whether sequence_step and sequence_prerequisite_ref are required
Determines whether conditions must have at least 2 entries (incompatible_combination)
feature_support_added restricts severity to info and warning only

fields.purpose: Tracks the current lifecycle state of the rule, controlling production eligibility, import permissions to Neo4j and Qdrant, and what operations are allowed on the rule.
validation_rules: Must be one of the 7 defined lifecycle states
Transitions must follow the allowed_transitions matrix
Archived is a terminal state and cannot transition to any other state
relationships_to_other_fields: When status = approved, approved_by and approved_at are both required
When status = approved, evidence must contain at least 1 entry
Only approved status permits Neo4j and Qdrant import

fields.purpose: Identifies the primary entity the rule applies to. The entity the compliance engine evaluates against the rule logic. Links to an RC2 canonical entity to enable graph traversal and version comparison.
fields.allowed_values: Object with entity_id (RC2 pattern ^[A-Z]{2,3}-[0-9]{3}$), component_name (string), and optional version_constraint object
validation_rules: entity_id must match ^[A-Z]{2,3}-[0-9]{3}$ and exist in RC2 registry
component_name must not be null or empty
version_constraint.operator must be from rule_type allowed operators
version_constraint.version_scheme must be one of: semantic, wildcard, named_release, calendar, unknown
relationships_to_other_fields: subject.entity_id must differ from object.entity_id
version_constraint.operator must be valid for the declared rule_type

fields.purpose: Declares the semantic relationship between subject and object. Drives the Neo4j edge type and semantic interpretation of the rule in compliance evaluation and root cause tracing.
validation_rules: Must be one of the 20 registered relationship names from compatibility_relationships.json
Must not be null or empty
Must be semantically appropriate for the rule_type
relationships_to_other_fields: Determines the Neo4j edge type between subject and object nodes
FIXED_BY is semantically aligned with known_issue_fixed rule_type
REQUIRES is semantically aligned with min_version_constraint and readiness_requirement

fields.purpose: Identifies the secondary entity the rule relates to via the predicate. The target of the semantic relationship. Same structure as subject.
fields.allowed_values: Object with entity_id (RC2 pattern), component_name (string), and optional version_constraint object
validation_rules: entity_id must match ^[A-Z]{2,3}-[0-9]{3}$ and exist in RC2 registry
component_name must not be null or empty
object.entity_id must not equal subject.entity_id
relationships_to_other_fields: object.entity_id must differ from subject.entity_id
version_constraint structure mirrors subject version_constraint validation rules

fields.purpose: Provides the scoping conditions that must be satisfied for the rule to evaluate. Restricts rule firing to specific system states, installed versions, or migration contexts. Empty for universal rules.
fields.allowed_values: Array of condition objects, each with condition_id, entity_id, component_name, operator, version_normalized, version_scheme, and optional condition_context. Can be empty array.
validation_rules: Can be empty array for universal rules
incompatible_combination rules must have at least 2 entries
Each condition_id must be unique within the array
Each entity_id must exist in the RC2 registry
relationships_to_other_fields: condition_logic determines how multiple conditions are combined (AND/OR)
incompatible_combination requires at least 2 conditions (enforced by VR-011)
Conditions reference entity_ids that must be in RC2 registry (same as subject and object)
